summary: Matched the first ftcliffcommon assembly block by replacing asm/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.s with src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.c and wiring the new C object into the build. The work added a dedicated ftcliffcommon header, many needed function prototypes, a typed lbl_804D6554 ftCommonData extern, and expanded Fighter-related struct coverage for cliff/ledge behavior. The matched functions cover ledge/cliff entry checks, cliff state setup, position snapping to cliff geometry, ledge cooldown handling, and camera/collision flag updates.
what_changed: Deleted the 314-line assembly file asm/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.s and added a 132-line C implementation in src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.c.
Implemented func_80081298, func_80081370, func_80081504, func_80081540, func_80081544, func_800815E4, and func_80081644 in C, matching the deleted assembly range shown in the diff excerpt.
Added src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.h with prototypes for the new C functions exposed from the module.
Switched obj_files.mk from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.o and added ftcliffcommon.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.

Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h with fields needed by the cliff code, including xC, x624, x68C, x890 CameraBox, x2064_ledgeCooldown, x213C, x221D_flag, x221F_flag, x2340_stateVar1, and related stateVar slots.
Added a CameraBox struct in fighter.h so func_80081644 can set fighter->x890->xC_flag.b0.
Typed lbl_804D6554 in include/variables.h as extern struct _ftCommonData* lbl_804D6554, replacing an unknown commented extern.

Added or corrected function prototypes in include/functions.h for callees used by ftcliffcommon.c, including func_8003FFDC, func_80040048, func_80053DA4, func_80053ECC, efAsync_Spawn, func_8007D5D4, func_8007D780, func_8007E2F4, func_8007E2FC, func_8007EBAC, func_8007EFC0, func_80082B1C, func_80082E3C, func_80088148, func_800881D8, func_8009A804, func_8009EF68, and func_800CC730.
Removed a local duplicate lbl_804D6554/UnkFighterStruct definition from ftsandbag.c and removed a local extern for func_8007E2F4 from ftkoopa.c because shared declarations now exist.
smart_moves: Centralized lbl_804D6554 as ftCommonData in variables.h instead of keeping per-file ad hoc extern structs, which made the cliff code and Sandbag code share one declaration.
Promoted previously unknown function declarations to typed prototypes only as needed by the matched C, reducing local extern clutter and improving type-checking across fighter code.
Kept offset-based field names such as x2340_stateVar1 and x2064_ledgeCooldown when semantics were partly known, preserving matching precision without over-naming uncertain fields.
Added explicit struct fields at assembly-observed offsets rather than using raw pointer arithmetic everywhere, making the C code readable while still matching known layout offsets.
Used build-system replacement rather than keeping the old asm object alongside the new C object, making the decompiled module the canonical source for that object.
decomp_lessons: When matching a fighter action/common module, expect to unblock the C by expanding Fighter layout fields at exact offsets used by the assembly, especially bitflag bytes and collision-related pointers.
Small module matches often require project-wide header cleanup: function prototypes, global variables, and local extern removal can be part of the matching PR, not just the new C file.

A shared common-data pointer such as lbl_804D6554 should be represented in include/variables.h with the best available struct type so multiple files do not invent incompatible local placeholder structs.

For uncertain data, offset-preserving names like x624, x213C, and x2340_stateVar1 are acceptable evidence-grounded placeholders; add semantic suffixes only where behavior is directly observed, such as x2064_ledgeCooldown.
Build files must be updated in both normal object lists and epilogue/ordering lists when replacing asm with C, otherwise the matched source may not be linked in the same position.
naming_conventions: New module header follows the file name: src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.h for src/melee/ft/ftcliffcommon.c.
Function names remain address-based placeholders such as func_80081298 and func_80081370 because no stronger symbolic names were present in the PR evidence.
Fighter fields use offset-prefixed names, for example x2C_facing_direction, x6F0_collData, x2064_ledgeCooldown, and x2340_stateVar1.
Bitfield wrapper names follow existing-style flag containers such as x221D_flag.bits.b7, x221F_flag.bits.b4, x2228_flag.bits.b2, and CameraBox xC_flag.b0.
Common data is named ftCommonData in fighter.h and exported through lbl_804D6554 as struct _ftCommonData*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the deleted assembly labels lbl_804D8378, lbl_804D837C, and lbl_804D8380 with C float literals 1.0f, -1.0f, and 0.0f in control flow that chooses cliff/ledge direction.
Matched branchy ledge logic in func_80081298 with early returns for the downward-speed/common-data threshold and collision envFlags checks.
Represented envFlags masks from the assembly as x6F0_collData.x134_envFlags checks against 0x03000000 and 0x01000000.
Preserved stack-shape pressure where needed with dummy locals and small local arrays, including unused integer locals, UnkParameterStruct, and sp10 arrays for ledge-position helper calls.
Converted assembly bit operations on fighter flags into bitfield assignments and checks, such as fighter->x221D_flag.bits.b7 = 1 and fighter->x2228_flag.bits.b2.
Selected left/right cliff geometry helpers based on facing direction: func_80053ECC for positive facing direction and func_80053DA4 otherwise.
Kept effect spawning through efAsync_Spawn with a pointer derived from gobj->user_data + 0x60C, matching the assembly's offset-based argument construction.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: If reindenting fighter.h, use 4 spaces like the other files.

review_feedback.author: JohnnyUrosevic
review_feedback.feedback: Author agreed and noted the indentation choice followed existing style in that file.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80081298, func_80081370, func_80081544, or other ftcliffcommon functions to semantic names.
Check subsequent fighter.h cleanup PRs for indentation/style fixes after r-burns's 4-space review comment.
Search for later ftCommonData expansions that replace filler around x480, x498_ledgeCooldownTime, or x5F0 with named fields.
Look for later refactors of x2340_stateVar1, x213C, x624, x68C, and CameraBox to confirm their final meanings.
Search later PRs touching efAsync_Spawn prototypes to see whether the varargs signature was refined.
